Overview

This article is about the original location in Fortnite: Battle Royale. For the version in Blitz Royale, see Blitz MEGA City.
This article is about a location that has been removed from the map.
Assets from this POI are available in UEFN/Creative
Assets from this POI are available in UEFN and Fortnite Creative inside of Prefabs and Galleries.

MEGA City (or Mega City) was a Named Location in Fortnite: Battle Royale that was added in Chapter 4: Season 2 to the island Asteria.

MEGA City was a vast, modern metropolis that resembled a futuristic depiction of Tokyo. Technologically advanced cars and motorcycles could have been found throughout its streets, while buildings across the city were encircled by Grind Rails.

History

History[]

  • C4S2
  • C4S3
  • C4S4
  • C4SOG

Update v24.00[]

March 10th 2023

  • MEGA City was brought by the Sideways Rift in the sky from the Rift Gate.
  • Stray spawns here as a Character.
  • Highcard may spawn here as a Boss.

Update v24.10[]

March 29th 2023

  • Criminal posters for Triarch Nox, Styx and Dahlia were added.
  • New sound effects for the fish hologram and 6 new songs were added in some locations.

Update v24.20[]

April 11th 2023

  • Scout Regiment Footlockers were added.

Update v24.40[]

May 17th 2023

  • One of the billboards on the 繁華街 Tower was changed for the Across the Spider-Verse Collaboration.
  • Bounty Boards were vaulted.

Update v25.00[]

June 9th 2023

  • Job Boards were added.
  • Scout Regiment Footlockers were vaulted.
  • Stray no longer spawns here as a Character.

Update v25.10[]

June 20th 2023

  • The Across the Spider-Verse billboard was changed back.

Update v25.20[]

July 26th 2023

  • The Planet Express Ship crashed into one of the billboards on the 乞うご Tower.
    • The billboard displays now a glitched image of Hypnotoad.
  • Bender Bending Rodríguez now spawns here as a Character.

Update v25.30[]

August 8th 2023

  • The Planet Express Ship disappeared and the billboard is back to its pre-v25.20 state.
  • Bender Bending Rodríguez no longer spawns here as a Character.

Update v26.00[]

August 25th 2023

  • 繁華街 Tower was taken over by the Eclipse.
  • Clubs Dealer was added as a Boss in the 繁華街 Tower with Low Cards
  • Nitro Drifters were vaulted.
  • Nitro Fangs can now spawn here.
  • The Cold Blooded Vault is now open and mostly empty.
  • Highcard and his henchmen were removed.
  • MEGAメガ Tower Penthouse was updated.
  • Floor in the Office Tower is no longer made out of wood.

Update v26.10[]

September 19th 2023

  • A Birthday Cake was added for Fortnite's 6th Birthday.

Update v26.20[]

September 26th 2023

  • The Birthday Cake was removed as Fortnite's 6th Birthday ended.

Update v26.30[]

October 25th 2023

  • Some billboards were changed to show an oddly familiar image of the sky. Going near any of them will play the OG Battle Royale theme.

Update v27.00[]

November 3rd 2023

  • MEGA City is no longer accessible due to time travel.

Trivia

Trivia[]

  • At the time, MEGA City was one of the biggest POI ever added.
    • This does not take into account Rickety Rig, whose territory was divided into 3 different large islands separated by the sea.
    • Over the years more and more huge locations were introduced to the game, like Spooky Suburbs on Reload's Nitemare Island or Battlewood Boulevard on Chapter 7's Golden Coast.
  • MEGA City and the bay area around it appear to be based on Tokyo Bay[1].
  • The Grind Rails found in MEGA City are most similar to the Ride Rails in Splatoon, the Grind Rails in Sonic, or the Grind Rails from Ratchet and Clank.
  • Before Chapter 4: Season 3, MEGA City was the only place on the island where it could rain.
  • Most of the levels within the city's taller skyscrapers are inaccessible, aside from the rooftop and bottom floors.
  • MEGA City is Evie's hometown[2] and came from Reality: 783, which was properly introduced during Chapter 6.
  • Some of the music throughout the location is made by MetaSounds.
  • Before Chapter 4: Season 4, MEGA City was the only Named POI on the map that didn't have a Capture Point.
  • The Tiger Statue breathes fire every Storm Circle.
  • MEGA City holds two possible references to the Mega Man franchise.
    • MEGA City shares the same name with a location from the Mega Man series, mainly in printed media.
    • The Tiger Statue heavily resembles Gospel from the Mega Man Battle Network series.
